content.full_text:
CalifOrnia
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
August 15, 199 O
Lois M. Shade
811 E. Juanita Avenue
Glendora, CA 917 40
Re: Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-9O-449
Dear Ms. Shade:
This is to confirm telephone advice provided to you
concerning your obligations regarding surplus campaign funds under
the Political ReforD Act (the "Act; . t
OUESTTONS2
1. 
May you retain your surplus campaign funds for your use
in a future carnpaign?
2. 
May the surplus campaign funds be used to publish a
newsletter providing only general information to the Glendora com-
rnunity?
3. 
What is the last date for disposing of surplus campaign
funds?
4. 
uay the surplus campaign funds be retained until the
organization to which you wish to contribute achieves the
requisite tax-exenpt, charitable status?
1&2. 
Once campa j.gn
their use. Perrnissible
CONCLUSION
funds become surplus
use of surplus funds funds, the Act linits
raised after January
1 cor"rrr*"nt code Sections 81ooo-91015' ALl statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
commission
regulations appear at 2 caLifornia Code of Regulations. Section
18500, et seq.- Al1 references to regulations are to Title 2,
oivision o of tfre California Code of Regulations.
')" These questions are formulated based upon our two telephone
conversations of June 28, L9gO, and your letter of the same date'
42g J Street, Suite 8O0 . P.o. Box 8O7 o sacramento cA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322'5660


our File No. A-9o-449
Paqe 2
L, 1990, does not include funding your future carnpaign or
publishing a neldsLetter.
3. 
Surplus funds need not be disposed of by any date
certain.
4. 
Surplus funds may be retained until the intended
recipient organization achieves tax-exempt status so long as
carnpaign statements are regularly filed.
FACTS
You presented the following facts in our tvro telephone
conversations of June 2A, ]-990. You are an unsuccessful candidate
for city council. The election was held in April 1990. You have
funds remaining in your carnpaign account.
ANALYSIS
Carnpaign funds under the control, of a candidate become
surplus funds at the end of the postelection reporting period fol-
lowing the defeat of the candidate. (Section 85807.)r In your
case the postelection period ended on June 30, 1990. (Section
84200.) once canpaign funds becone surplus, their use is lirnited.
Under Section 85807, surplus carnpaign funds can be used only for
the following purposes:
(a) The payment of outstandi.ng campaign
debts or elected officer's 
expenses.
(b) The pro rata repayment of
contributions.
(c) Donations to any bona fide
charitable, educational, civic, religious, or
sinifar tax-exempt, nonprofit organization,
where no substantial part of the proceeds will
have a rnaterial financial effect on the forner
candidate or elected officer, any nember of
his or her inmediate farnily, or his or her
campaign treasurer.
(d) contributions to a political party
or connittee so long as the funds are not used
to nake contributions in support of or
opposition to a candi.date for elective office'
(e) Contributions to support or oppose
any candidate for federal office, any
3 
Th" comnission staff intends to present a regiulation to the
coronission in the near future tlrat wilt clarify the provisions of
Section 85807.


Our File No. A-9O-449
Page 3
candidate for elective office in a state other
than california, or any ba}lot measure.
No other use is permitted. Thus you may not retain the funds
for your future campaign or to publish a newsletter.
The Act does not specify a date certain that you must
dispose of the surplus campaign funds. However, so long as
the canpaign account remains active, you must abide by the
filinq requirement of the Act. 
(see sections a42oo-a4226.)
Thus you rnay retain your surplus carnpaign funds untif the
organization to hrhich you wish to contribute achieves the
requisite tax-exempt, nonprofit status.
I trust that this letter has provided you t ith the
guidance requested. If you have any further questions
- tegardinq this natter, please contact me at (916) 322-59oL.
s incere ly,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
呼
聰r
Counsel, Legal Division
SH:MM:dg
